evals.prompt: Define color design tokens for a project with light and dark mode support.
evals.expected_output: Uses @layer config, OKLCH colors, light-dark() for theme-sensitive tokens, and strict text-/bg-/border- naming.
expectations: Uses OKLCH for all color values
Uses light-dark() for tokens that differ between light and dark mode
Names color tokens with text-, bg-, or border- prefix only — no brand-, surface-, or palette- names
Does not use @media (prefers-color-scheme) to define token overrides

evals.prompt: Build a button component in CSS.
evals.expected_output: Uses @layer components, c- prefix, BEM naming, and only token references — no hardcoded values.
expectations: Uses c- prefix on the component class (e.g. .c-button)
References only CSS custom properties — no hardcoded colors, sizes, or font values
Uses logical properties for padding (padding-block, padding-inline)

evals.prompt: Set up user-toggleable light and dark themes.
evals.expected_output: Uses light-dark() in config tokens and flips color-scheme via [data-theme] attribute — no repeated token values.
expectations: Does not repeat token values for dark mode
Uses [data-theme='dark'] { color-scheme: dark } to trigger dark mode
Uses [data-theme='light'] { color-scheme: light } for light mode override
Does not use @media (prefers-color-scheme) as a manual theme override mechanism

evals.prompt: Declare the CSS layer order for a new project.
evals.expected_output: Declares all layers upfront in the correct order: config, resets, components, utilities, overrides.
expectations: Uses @layer to declare all layers at the top of the main CSS file
Includes config, resets, components, utilities, and overrides in that order
Does not use Tailwind, Bootstrap, or any CSS framework
